sections.title: Vision
sections.intro: A web-based engineering cockpit that lets a single human operator run a team of AI agents collaborating on software projects. The agents debate designs, divide work, execute tasks in parallel, review each other's PRs, and merge — all coordinated through the application. The human is the customer: they set direction, make final decisions, and steer when needed.

sections.title: Team Model
sections.intro: The application models a software development team with four distinct roles: Customer (human operator), Analyst (Claude — paid), Team Lead (Ollama — free), Developer (Claude/Codex/Copilot — paid).

requirements.text: The system shall support a group debate where the customer poses a requirement and agents discuss it in round-robin turns. Each agent gets one turn per round. The debate runs for up to N rounds (default 20) or until agreement is reached.

tests.description: Round-robin cycles through all agents in turn_order, then increments round
Skips disabled agents (enabled=0) in the rotation
is_complete() returns true after max_rounds rounds
State correctly resets when starting a new debate session
Reaching max_rounds sends summary prompt to all agents
Structured summary responses are collected and formatted into comparison
System notice with comparison is emitted and gate set to pending_approval
Customer message appears in all agents' context on their next turn
Customer message does not advance the turn counter or round
Multiple customer messages between turns are all preserved in order

requirements.text: The customer is a participant in the debate. Agents shall be able to @ the customer for clarifications. The system shall notify the customer and pause the debate until they respond.

tests.description: Customer message appears in all agents' context on their next turn
Customer message does not advance the turn counter or round
Multiple customer messages between turns are all preserved in order
Agent response with @Customer triggers paused state and clarification_needed event
No turns are dispatched while debate is paused
Customer response unpauses debate and next agent in order proceeds

requirements.text: The customer shall be able to direct a message to a specific agent by name (e.g., "rick, what do you think about option 2?"). The addressed agent responds next, regardless of round-robin order.

tests.description: Directed message gives targeted agent immediate next turn
Round-robin resumes from interrupted position after directed turn
Directed message mid-round does not cause any agent to be skipped

requirements.text: If agents agree on an option, they shall present it to the customer for final approval. The customer's decision is always final — agreement alone does not advance the phase.

tests.description: Structured JSON agreement marker is parsed correctly from agent response
Text-based agreement phrases are detected as fallback
Unanimous agreement emits system_notice and sets gate to pending_approval
Partial agreement (2/3 agents) does not trigger gate change

requirements.text: If agents do not agree after N rounds, they shall summarize each position with pros/cons and ask the customer to decide or steer the discussion.

tests.description: Reaching max_rounds sends summary prompt to all agents
Structured summary responses are collected and formatted into comparison
System notice with comparison is emitted and gate set to pending_approval

requirements.text: Once the customer approves a direction, they shall @ a specific agent (typically the Analyst or Team Lead) to break the agreed design into a task list, identifying which tasks can run in parallel.

tests.description: Valid structured task list JSON is parsed into Task records
Tasks with no dependencies are identified as parallelizable
Invalid task JSON format returns descriptive error
Only tasks with all dependencies done are returned as assignable
Idle agent gets highest-priority available task assigned
Agent already working on a task does not get a second assignment
Assignment updates task owner_agent_id, task status, and agent status

requirements.text: The task list shall include for each task: title, description, scope (files/modules), dependencies on other tasks, and estimated complexity.

tests.description: Valid work order transitions succeed (design -> tdd -> implementing -> etc.)
Invalid transition (e.g., design -> implementing skipping tdd) raises error
testing -> implementing is allowed (test failure rollback)
Configurable work order can skip TDD step for docs tasks

requirements.text: Each Developer agent shall work on its assigned task independently, following the work order: design doc → TDD → implement → test → debug/fix → update docs → raise PR.

tests.description: Valid work order transitions succeed (design -> tdd -> implementing -> etc.)
Invalid transition (e.g., design -> implementing skipping tdd) raises error
testing -> implementing is allowed (test failure rollback)
Configurable work order can skip TDD step for docs tasks

requirements.text: Each agent shall work in its own isolated git worktree to prevent file conflicts with other agents.

tests.description: create_worktree produces correct path and branch name from task slug and provider
cleanup_worktree removes worktree directory and deletes branch
Worktree base directory is configurable and defaults to ../agent-orchestrator-worktrees/

requirements.text: The customer shall be able to steer a specific agent mid-task if they observe it going off-track.

tests.description: Steering notes appear in agent prompt prefix when pending
Batch pauses after batch_size turns and records scheduler_run as completed
Continue from paused state resumes with a new batch
Directed steer message (@AgentName) targets specific agent

requirements.text: When a PR is raised, the system shall assign a reviewer — any Developer agent that did not author the PR.

tests.description: Author agent is never assigned as reviewer of own PR
Idle agent with lightest review load is selected as reviewer
Single-developer conversation flags PR for customer review

requirements.text: The PR author shall automatically fix review comments and push updates. The reviewer shall re-review until satisfied, then approve.

tests.description: PR raised triggers automatic reviewer assignment by Team Lead
Review comments transition task to fixing_comments status
Approval enters PR into merge queue with correct position
Re-review after fixes cycles back through review correctly
Agent with comments on own PR gets fix_comments as highest priority action
Agent with approved PR and no comments gets merge action
Agent assigned as reviewer gets review action when no own PR work needed
Agent with no work returns idle action

requirements.text: Merges to main shall be serialized — one PR at a time. The system shall maintain a merge queue and handle rebases automatically.

tests.description: Queue enforces FIFO: first-approved PR is first to process
Only one PR can be in rebasing/testing/merging at any time
Customer re-prioritization changes queue order
Full status lifecycle: queued -> rebasing -> testing -> merging -> merged

requirements.text: Other agents shall wait while one agent is merging. The merge coordinator (Team Lead / Ollama) shall manage this queue at zero token cost to paid agents.

tests.description: Clean rebase advances PR status from rebasing to testing
Rebase conflict marks PR as blocked and emits notification
CI pass triggers merge with configured strategy (squash default)
While one PR is merging, no other PR can enter rebasing/testing/merging
Coordinator loop advances next queued PR when no active merge exists
Event trigger (PR approved) causes immediate queue evaluation
Rebase conflict marks PR as blocked and notifies author + customer
Merge timeout (>10 min) alerts customer

requirements.text: After raising a PR, an agent shall follow this priority loop: 1) Check if any of its own PRs have review comments → fix them. 2) Check if its PR got approved → merge it (if merge queue allows). 3) Check if it has been tagged to review another agent's PR → review it. 4) Otherwise → wait for next assignment or pick up next task from queue.

tests.description: Agent with comments on own PR gets fix_comments as highest priority action
Agent with approved PR and no comments gets merge action
Agent assigned as reviewer gets review action when no own PR work needed
Agent with no work returns idle action

requirements.text: The system shall provide a real-time activity viewer for each agent — showing its thinking, file edits, commands, and tool use. This viewer shall be show/hide toggleable and separate from the main chat.

tests.description: Renders with terminal aesthetic: dark background, monospace font
Renders thinking event as dimmed italic text
Renders tool_call event with highlighted tool name badge and tool_result indented below
Shows Resume button when user scrolls up; clicking it re-enables auto-scroll
Connects to SSE endpoint and receives parsed events
Reconnects with Last-Event-ID header after connection error
Closes EventSource on component unmount (cleanup)
Exposes connection status (connecting, open, closed, error)
Connects to agent activity SSE endpoint when agent ID is provided
Parses typed activity events (thinking, tool_call, error)
Disconnects and clears events when agent ID changes
Defaults to showing AgentRoster in right pane
Switches to ActivityViewer when an agent is selected
Back to Roster button returns to roster view

requirements.text: The main chat window shall remain clean — only deliberate messages, turn outputs, and status updates appear there. Internal agent activity (thinking, tool calls) shall only appear in the activity viewer.

tests.description: Renders agent message with avatar showing first letter and hash-based color
Renders markdown content (code block, bold, link) via react-markdown
Renders phase_change type as a styled divider with phase label
Renders steer message with highlight border and 'You' sender label
Fetches initial messages from API on mount
Appends new messages from SSE stream in real-time
Deduplicates messages by ID when SSE delivers already-fetched messages

requirements.text: The system shall provide a dashboard view showing: which agent is working on which task, task status (design, TDD, implementing, testing, PR raised, in review, fixing comments, merging, done), PR links, and merge queue order.

tests.description: Renders all 10 status columns with correct headers
Shows task count in each column header
Empty columns have dimmed styling
Tasks render in the correct column based on status
Renders KanbanBoard, AgentStatusBar, and MergeQueue sections
Opens TaskDetailModal when a task card is clicked
Renders empty state when no tasks exist
Renders task title, agent badge, and status accent border
Long title is truncated with tooltip showing full text
PR link renders as clickable link opening in new tab when PR exists
Clicking card calls onClick handler with task ID

requirements.text: The dashboard shall be accessible from within a conversation — the user shall be able to switch between chat view and dashboard view (layout TBD).

tests.description: Renders Chat and Dashboard toggle buttons in TopBar
Clicking Dashboard shows KanbanBoard in center pane
Clicking Chat returns to ChatPane with messages preserved

requirements.text: The system shall run locally on the user's machine. No cloud hosting required. LAN-accessible for multi-device use (phone, tablet, laptop).

tests.description: GET /api/conversations/{id} returns full details with agent_count and task_count
GET /api/conversations/{id} returns 404 for non-existent or soft-deleted conversation
PATCH /api/conversations/{id} updates title and project_path, returns updated object
PATCH /api/conversations/{id} ignores null fields and returns 404 for missing conversation
POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages creates a customer message event and returns it
POST with target_agent_id stores directed message metadata
GET /api/conversations/{id}/messages returns paginated results with ?after cursor
GET /api/conversations/{id}/messages returns 404 for non-existent conversation
GET /api/conversations/{id}/events returns all events unfiltered
GET with ?event_type=chat_message filters correctly
GET with multiple event_type values (comma-separated) returns matching events
POST /api/conversations/{id}/tasks creates a task with spec_json and returns it
GET /api/conversations/{id}/tasks lists all tasks ordered by priority
PATCH /api/tasks/{id} updates status through valid transitions
PATCH /api/tasks/{id} rejects invalid status transitions with 400
GET /api/conversations/{id}/phase returns current phase and gate_status
POST /gate with approve advances phase to next in sequence
POST /gate with reject sets gate_status to rejected, phase unchanged
POST /gate on a done conversation returns 409
POST /merge-queue enqueues item at next position with status queued
GET /merge-queue returns items ordered by position
PATCH /merge-queue/{id} rejects merging when another item is already merging
DELETE /merge-queue/{id} removes item and recompacts positions
SSE stream delivers events in correct SSE envelope format
Last-Event-ID header causes replay of missed events
Heartbeat comments are sent to keep connection alive
Stream returns 404 for non-existent conversation
Agent activity stream delivers only events for the specified agent
Activity stream filters to activity-related event types
Agent activity stream supports Last-Event-ID reconnection
Unhandled exceptions return 500 with envelope format and no stack trace
Pydantic ValidationError returns 400 with field-level error details
Custom NotFoundError returns 404 with envelope
Custom ConflictError returns 409 with envelope
GET /api/agents/{id}/status returns current status
PATCH /api/agents/{id}/status accepts valid transition idle -> running
PATCH /api/agents/{id}/status rejects invalid transition idle -> blocked
Status change emits agent_status_change system event
POST /api/conversations/{id}/agents links agent with auto-assigned turn_order
Duplicate agent assignment returns 409 Conflict
is_merge_coordinator flag is stored and returned correctly

requirements.text: The Team Lead role (Ollama) shall be free — no paid API calls for coordination, queue management, or status polling.

tests.description: Ollama adapter uses HTTP API (POST /api/generate) not CLI subprocess
Streaming HTTP response is parsed into AdapterEvent stream
is_available() checks /api/tags endpoint and returns bool
Model is configurable and passed correctly in API request
Coordinator loop advances next queued PR when no active merge exists
Event trigger (PR approved) causes immediate queue evaluation
Rebase conflict marks PR as blocked and notifies author + customer
Merge timeout (>10 min) alerts customer

requirements.text: Paid agent tokens shall only be spent on productive work: reasoning, code generation, code review, debate. Never on polling, queue checking, or coordination overhead.

tests.description: Ollama adapter uses HTTP API (POST /api/generate) not CLI subprocess
Streaming HTTP response is parsed into AdapterEvent stream
is_available() checks /api/tags endpoint and returns bool
Model is configurable and passed correctly in API request
Only tasks with all dependencies done are returned as assignable
Idle agent gets highest-priority available task assigned
Agent already working on a task does not get a second assignment
Assignment updates task owner_agent_id, task status, and agent status
